text: This video is just showing the insertion of a chest strain after someone's had surgery on their lungs.

Naturally the body wants to produce a lot of fluid after it's been injured but one of the problems with this when it's involving the chest is it actually makes it quite difficult to breathe afterwards.

So what happens is the surgeon places a big tube which is hollow on the inside at the bottom of the lungs and what that will do over the next few days while the patient's recovering is it'll just drain out any excess fluid and make it easier for them to breathe and recover.
